questionText: Complete the sentence: You will not pass the exam ______ you study harder.
options: if
unless
provided that
as long as
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'unless'. 'Unless' signifie 'sauf si' ou 'à moins que'. La phrase signifie : 'Tu ne réussiras pas l'examen à moins que tu étudies plus.' On ne peut pas utiliser 'if' car cela inverserait le sens, et les autres options expriment une condition positive.

questionText: Which sentence means the same as: 'If you don't leave now, you will be late.'?
options: If you leave now, you will be late.
Unless you leave now, you will be late.
Unless you leave now, you won't be late.
If you leave now, you won't be late.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'Unless you leave now, you will be late.' 'Unless' remplace 'if not'. La structure est: Unless + condition positive (leave now) + conséquence négative (will be late).

questionText: Transform the sentence using 'unless': 'We will cancel the picnic if it rains.'
options: Unless it rains, we will cancel the picnic.
We will cancel the picnic unless it doesn't rain.
We will cancel the picnic unless it rains.
Unless it doesn't rain, we will cancel the picnic.
explanation: La transformation correcte est 'We will cancel the picnic unless it rains.' La phrase originale signifie: Nous annulerons le pique-nique s'il pleut. 'Unless it rains' = 'sauf s'il pleut' ou 'à moins qu'il ne pleuve'. L'option A inverserait le sens.

questionText: Which conditional structure is used for a general truth or scientific fact?
options: If + present simple, will + base verb
If + past simple, would + base verb
If + present simple, present simple
If + past perfect, would have + past participle
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'If + present simple, present simple'. Il s'agit du conditionnel de type 0 (Type 0 conditional), utilisé pour exprimer des vérités générales ou des lois scientifiques (ex: If you heat water, it boils).

questionText: Identify the Type 1 conditional sentence.
options: If I were rich, I would buy a car.
If it rains tomorrow, we will stay home.
If she had studied, she would have passed.
If you freeze water, it becomes ice.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'If it rains tomorrow, we will stay home.' C'est un conditionnel de type 1 (réel/probable) : If + present simple (it rains) + will + base verb (will stay). Il exprime une situation possible dans le futur.

questionText: What is the correct inverted form of 'If I were you, I would apologize.'?
options: Were I you, I would apologize.
If were I you, I would apologize.
I were you, I would apologize.
Would I be you, I apologize.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'Were I you, I would apologize.' C'est l'inversion du conditionnel de type 2 pour 'if I were'. On supprime 'if' et on inverse le sujet et le verbe 'were'. Cette construction est formelle et élégante.

questionText: Choose the correct option: '______ had I known about the accident, I would have helped.'
options: If
Unless
Had
Were
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'Had'. Il s'agit de l'inversion du conditionnel de type 3 pour 'If I had known'. On supprime 'if' et on inverse 'had' avec le sujet : 'Had I known...' = 'Si j'avais su...'.

questionText: Complete the sentence: 'You can go to the party ______ you come back before midnight.'
options: unless
if not
provided that
otherwise
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'provided that'. 'Provided that' signifie 'à condition que'. C'est un substitut de 'if' qui insiste sur la condition nécessaire. La phrase signifie : 'Tu peux aller à la fête à condition que tu reviennes avant minuit'.

questionText: Transform using 'otherwise': 'You must wear a helmet. If not, you cannot ride the motorcycle.'
options: You must wear a helmet, otherwise you cannot ride the motorcycle.
Otherwise you must wear a helmet, you cannot ride the motorcycle.
You must wear a helmet unless you cannot ride the motorcycle.
You cannot ride the motorcycle otherwise you wear a helmet.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'You must wear a helmet, otherwise you cannot ride the motorcycle.' 'Otherwise' signifie 'sinon' et relie deux propositions où la seconde est la conséquence de la non-réalisation de la première.

questionText: Choose the correct 'if substitute' for this sentence: 'I will lend you my car on condition that you drive carefully.'
options: unless
otherwise
as long as
even if
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'as long as'. 'As long as' signifie 'aussi longtemps que' ou 'à condition que'. Il exprime une condition nécessaire. 'On condition that' est synonyme de 'as long as' ou 'provided that'.

questionText: Which sentence correctly uses 'unless'?
options: Unless you will study, you will fail.
Unless you study, you will fail.
Unless you don't study, you will fail.
You will fail unless you don't study.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'Unless you study, you will fail.' Après 'unless', on utilise le présent simple, pas le futur. 'Unless' contient déjà une négation, donc on n'ajoute pas 'don't/doesn't'.

questionText: What does 'Were she my daughter, I would be proud' mean?
options: Si elle était ma fille, je serais fière.
Si elle est ma fille, je serai fière.
À moins qu'elle soit ma fille, je serai fière.
Elle est ma fille et je suis fière.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'Si elle était ma fille, je serais fière.' C'est l'inversion formelle du conditionnel de type 2 (hypothétique). 'Were she my daughter' = 'If she were my daughter'.

questionText: Complete the sentence: '______ you finish your homework, you can watch TV.'
options: Unless
If not
As soon as
Even if
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'As soon as'. 'As soon as' signifie 'dès que'. Ce n'est pas exactement un substitut de 'if', mais une conjonction de temps qui peut exprimer une condition temporelle. La phrase : 'Dès que tu auras fini tes devoirs, tu peux regarder la TV'.

questionText: Which of these is NOT a correct substitute for 'if not'?
options: unless
provided that not
but for
without
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'provided that not'. 'Provided that' est positif (à condition que). Pour exprimer 'if not' on utilise 'unless' ou 'but for' (sans) ou 'without' (sans). 'Provided that not' n'est pas une construction correcte.

questionText: Transform this sentence using inversion: 'If the company should fail, we will lose our jobs.'
options: Should the company fail, we will lose our jobs.
The company should fail, we will lose our jobs.
Had the company failed, we would lose our jobs.
Were the company to fail, we lose our jobs.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'Should the company fail, we will lose our jobs.' C'est l'inversion du conditionnel de type 1 avec 'should' (éventualité). On supprime 'if' et on inverse 'should' avec le sujet. C'est une construction formelle.

questionText: Choose the correct option: 'The match will be cancelled ______ it stops raining.'
options: if
unless
without
except
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'unless'. La phrase signifie : 'Le match sera annulé à moins qu'il ne cesse de pleuvoir.' 'Unless it stops raining' = 'sauf s'il cesse de pleuvoir'. Si on utilisait 'if', le sens serait différent (si la pluie s'arrête, le match est maintenu).

questionText: What is the meaning of 'but for' in this sentence: 'But for your help, I would have failed'?
options: Mais pour
Sauf si
Sans
Grâce à
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'Sans'. 'But for' signifie 'sans' (littéralement 'à part pour'). La phrase signifie : 'Sans ton aide, j'aurais échoué.' C'est un substitut élégant de 'if it had not been for'.

questionText: Identify the Type 2 conditional sentence.
options: If you heat ice, it melts.
If she invites me, I will go.
If I had money, I would travel.
If he had arrived on time, he wouldn't have missed the train.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'If I had money, I would travel.' C'est un conditionnel de type 2 (irréel du présent) : If + past simple (had) + would + base verb (would travel). Il exprime une situation hypothétique ou imaginaire dans le présent/futur.

questionText: Complete the sentence using inversion: '______ he known the truth, he would have acted differently.'
options: Were
If
Unless
Had
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'Had'. C'est l'inversion du conditionnel de type 3. 'Had he known' = 'If he had known'. On utilise 'had' + sujet + participe passé pour former l'inversion dans les phrases conditionnelles au passé.

questionText: Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct with 'provided that'.
options: Provided that you will study, you will pass.
You will pass provided that you study.
Provided that you studied, you will pass.
You will pass provided that you will study.
explanation: La bonne réponse est 'You will pass provided that you study.' 'Provided that' (ou 'providing that') est suivi du présent simple, pas du futur, pour exprimer une condition future. La proposition principale utilise le futur.
